business.title: How emerging markets borrow: New evidence on sovereign bond issuance
business.description: Emerging market debt has surged since the pandemic, renewing concerns about rollover risk and fiscal vulnerability. This column uses a new auction-level dataset covering sovereign bond issuance events across 20 emerging markets to study the evolution, currency composition, and maturity of emerging market bond issuance. The majority of issuance is in local currency and has a maturity of five years or less. Furthermore, local-currency borrowing is mainly driven by rollover needs, while foreign-currency issuance is timed around global financial conditions. These findings underscore the importance of developing deep domestic bond markets as core infrastructure for macroeconomic resilience.

ai.title: OpenAI Isn’t Just Buying A Podcast It’s Buying Influence In The AI Narrative War
ai.description: INSIDE THIS REPORT A century-old strategy is quietly resurfacing as tech giants move to control not just platforms—but the conversations surrounding them. OpenAI’s acquisition of a niche but influential AI talk show signals a deeper shift in how power is exercised in the digital age. The question is no longer who builds the technology—but who OpenAI’s acquisition of TBPN signals a strategic move to shape AI narratives, reflecting a broader shift in how influence and technology intersect.
